title: Databricks SQL version 2025.30 is now available in Preview
body:
Databricks SQL version 2025.30 is now available in the **Preview** channel. Review the following section to learn about new features, behavioral changes, and bug fixes.

#### UTF8 based collations now support LIKE operator

You can now use LIKE with columns that have one of the following collations enabled: `UTF8_Binary`, `UTF8_Binary_RTRIM`, `UTF8_LCASE`, `UTF8_LCASE_RTRIM`. See [Collation](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-collation).

#### `ST_ExteriorRing` function is now supported

You can now use the `ST_ExteriorRing` function to extract the outer boundary of a polygon and return it as a linestring. See [`st_exteriorring` function](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/functions/st_exteriorring).

#### Declare multiple session or local variables in a single `DECLARE` statement

You can now declare multiple session or local variables of the same type and default value in a single `DECLARE` statement. See [DECLARE VARIABLE](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-syntax-ddl-declare-variable) and [BEGIN END compound statement](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/control-flow/compound-stmt).

#### Support `TEMPORARY` keyword for metric view creation

You can now use the `TEMPORARY` keyword when creating a metric view. Temporary metric views are visible only in the session that created them and are dropped when the session ends. See [CREATE VIEW](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-syntax-ddl-create-view).

#### `DESCRIBE CONNECTION` shows environment settings for JDBC connections

Databricks now includes user-defined environment settings in the [`DESCRIBE CONNECTION`](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/sql-ref-syntax-aux-describe-connection) output for JDBC connections that support custom drivers and run in isolation. Other connection types remain unchanged.

#### SQL syntax for Delta read options in streaming queries

You can now specify Delta read options for SQL-based streaming queries using the `WITH` clause. For example:

```
SELECT * FROM STREAM tbl WITH (SKIPCHANGECOMMITS=true, STARTINGVERSION=X);
```

#### Correct results for `split` with empty regex and positive limit

Databricks now returns correct results when using [`split function`](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/functions/split) with an empty regex and a positive limit. Previously, the function incorrectly truncated the remaining string instead of including it in the last element.

#### Fix `url_decode` and `try_url_decode` error handling in Photon

In Photon, [`try_url_decode()`](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/functions/try_url_decode) and [`url_decode()`](/aws/en/sql/language-manual/functions/url_decode) with `failOnError = false` now return `NULL` for invalid URL-encoded strings instead of failing the query.
